mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in PAYEMS signals improving labor income and expected revenues, causing investors to revise S&P 500 earnings expectations upward and producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2010-11-15 latest PAYEMS snapshot was 130462.0
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month PAYEMS changes fail to deliver higher mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than nonpositive changes with t-test p <= 0.05 over walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above its trailing 3-month average by more than 0.2 percentage points, labor-market deterioration raises recession and margin-risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its trailing 3-month average by > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2010-11-15 latest UNRATE snapshot was 9.6
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Joint positive 3-month changes in RSAFS and PAYEMS identify a demand recovery supported by job income rather than temporary spending, leading to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2010-11-15 latest RSAFS snapshot was 373103.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS-PAYEMS state does not outperform states where only one series is positive by at least 50 basis points over 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.5%, the discount-rate channel dominates short-run growth concerns because policy is highly accommodative, raising equity valuation multiples and producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2010-11-15 latest FEDFUNDS snapshot was 0.19
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS-below-0.5 triggered windows have lower 60-day forward returns than all other windows in at least two consecutive walk-forward test years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals stronger goods output and operating leverage, causing expected corporate profits to rise and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2010-11-15 latest INDPRO snapshot was 93.1902
failure_condition: Reject if the slope coefficient on 3-month INDPRO change is nonpositive in out-of-sample 20-day return regressions across two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below a 600 thousand annualized pace, weak residential construction signals impaired household credit creation and durable-goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2010-11-15 latest HOUST snapshot was 610.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-600 triggered observations show positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases and no drawdown-frequency increase versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, negative real short-rate pressure increases liquidity and nominal earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2010-11-15 CPIAUCSL was 218.372 and FEDFUNDS was 0.19
regime_hint: zero lower bound with positive inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-below-0.5 interaction has a negative out-of-sample coefficient for 20-day returns or underperforms either single condition alone.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises over 3 months while RSAFS falls over 3 months, production outpaces final demand and raises inventory or margin risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO-up and RSAFS-down states do not have lower average 5-day returns than all other states by at least 25 basis points in walk-forward tests.
